name: Las Palmas, Gran Canaria
summary: Las Palmas sits on Gran Canaria, a volcanic island in the Atlantic Ocean about 150 km west of Africa. The weather feels like spring all year, the city hugs golden Las Canteras beach, and Christopher Columbus stopped here in 1492 to fix a ship before sailing across the ocean.
funFacts: Christopher Columbus stopped on Gran Canaria in 1492 to repair a broken rudder before sailing west, and a museum called Casa de Colon now tells the story.
Gran Canaria is nicknamed a 'continent in miniature' because in one short drive you can pass beaches, pine forests, deserts and volcanic mountains.
The Maspalomas Dunes in the south are like a tiny Sahara meeting the sea, with golden sand stretching for several kilometres next to the Atlantic.
On nearby La Gomera island people talk by whistling a language called Silbo Gomero, loud enough to carry messages three kilometres across deep valleys, and UNESCO protects it.
The Gran Canaria giant lizard, Gallotia stehlini, lives only here and can grow up to 80 centimetres long, including its very long tail.
thingsToDo: Stroll the golden 3 km curve of Playa de Las Canteras and peek at La Barra, a natural reef that calms the waves and shelters fish.
Visit Casa de Colon in Vegueta, the colourful old town, to see maps, ship models and learn how Columbus prepared for his 1492 voyage.
Wander Vegueta's cobbled lanes to Santa Ana cathedral and climb the rooftop tower for a view across orange-tiled houses to the harbour.
Explore Poema del Mar aquarium near the cruise pier, with sharks, jellyfish and one of the largest curved viewing windows in the world.
Picnic in Parque Doramas and pop into Pueblo Canario to see traditional balconied houses, gardens, waterfalls and big shaded playgrounds.
Try Canarian papas arrugadas (wrinkly salty potatoes) with green mojo sauce at a Triana street cafe before heading back to the ship.

localWord.meaning: the local Canarian word for 'bus' instead of the usual Spanish 'autobus'

images.alt: A long sandy beach in Las Palmas with calm blue water and people walking along the shore.
Colourful historic streets and buildings in the old Vegueta district of Las Palmas.
The grand stone cathedral of Santa Ana rising above the plaza in old Las Palmas.
Golden sand dunes rolling like a tiny desert at Maspalomas on Gran Canaria.
The towering Roque Nublo rock standing tall above the green mountains of Gran Canaria.
